title: 10 best herbal creams for cold sore

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel inside the aloe leaf contains powerful antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that calm the skin and reduce redness and swelling associated with cold sores. When applied topically, the herbal extract forms a protective barrier on the skin's surface, shielding it from further irritation and promoting healing. This natural remedy provides relief from itching, burning, and discomfort, allowing the cold sore to heal faster and leave minimal scarring.
remedy_properties: Soothing Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, which help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores by soothing the affected area.

Antiviral Activity: Aloe vera contains antiviral compounds that can inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores, helping to prevent further spread and promote healing.

Hydrating Properties: Herbal aloe creams retain moisture and provide hydration to the skin, which helps to accelerate the healing process by reducing dryness and cracking associated with cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help soothe and reduce redness associated with cold sores.
Roots: Contain bioactive compounds like saponins and phenolic acids that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in combating the virus that causes cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from the leaf of the plant weighing around 500 grams.
Combine 250 milliliters of distilled water with 50 grams of grated licorice root powder in a heat-proof bowl.
Boil the mixture over medium flame for 10 minutes stirring occasionally then let cool.
Blend 200 milliliters of pure aloe vera gel with 20 milliliters of tea tree essential oil and 10 grams of zinc oxide powder.
Stir well the combined mixture until smooth then transfer it to a sterilized glass jar storing in refrigerator for up to one week.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with cold sore because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and soothe discomfort. The cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to calm the affected area, reducing redness and swelling associated with cold sores. Additionally, pot marigold's antibacterial properties aid in fighting off the underlying virus, while its emollient properties moisturize and protect the skin, accelerating the healing process and promoting a rapid recovery from cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and redness associated with cold sore outbreaks.
Antiviral properties: The cream's antiviral properties, including its ability to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), can help shorten the duration and severity of cold sores.
Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold's antioxidant and antibacterial compounds promote faster wound healing by reducing oxidative stress, preventing bacterial growth, and improving skin integrity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent antimicrobial properties to combat viral and bacterial infections associated with cold sores.
Leaves: Soothing and anti-inflammatory effects to reduce redness, swelling, and pain caused by cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers and dry them thoroughly for 7-10 days or until crisp.
Combine dried calendula flowers with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:4 for 4 hours.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container within 15 minutes.
Add beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 1:0.5 to thicken and emulsify the cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with cold sore because of its natural antiviral and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce the severity and duration of the outbreak. The cream's active ingredients, such as terpinen-4-ol and α-terpineol, have been shown to inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, reducing the likelihood of new lesions forming. Additionally, tea tree oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the affected area, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Tea tree oil contains compounds like terpinen-4-ol and alpha-terpineol, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), the virus that causes cold sores.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The menthol and camphor present in tea tree oil can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores, providing relief from discomfort.
Antimicrobial activity: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties can help prevent bacterial and fungal infections that may exacerbate or prolong the healing process of cold sores.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antimicrobial and antiviral properties, tea tree leaves are effective in combating the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores.
Seeds: Rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, tea tree seeds help soothe and calm the skin while also providing protection against infection.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 100g of high-quality beeswax pellets and add them to a double boiler.
Heat the beeswax until it melts completely taking about 10 minutes to reach temperature.
Add 30ml of fractionated coconut oil and 20ml of sweet almond oil to the melted beeswax mixture stirring well.
Combine 5g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and 2.5g of aloe vera powder in a small bowl then stir into the oil mixture.
Pour the mixture into sterilized tubes or jars and let it cool and set at room temperature for about 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with cold sore because of its unique anti-viral properties, which help to soothe and calm the affected area. The cream's active ingredient, Echinacea purpurea, contains potent compounds that target the herpes simplex virus, reducing its severity and duration. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing qualities promote healthy skin cell growth, accelerating the healing process and leaving the skin feeling smooth and hydrated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea contains compounds that reduce inflammation and swelling, which can help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with cold sores.
Antiviral properties: Echinacea has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against herpes simplex virus (HSV), which is the cause of cold sores.

Immunomodulatory properties: Echinacea extracts have been found to stimulate immune cells, such as natural killer cells and macrophages, which can help boost the body's defenses against viral infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm skin irritations associated with cold sores.
Fruits: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antiviral properties and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating cold sore symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm, dry place for at least 24 hours or air dry for several days.
Grind dried echinacea roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 2 tablespoons of ground echinacea powder with 6 tablespoons of sweet almond oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture over low heat for about 20 minutes until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The soothing agent in chamomile, apigenin, calms the affected area, reducing redness and swelling associated with cold sores. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial compounds inhibit the growth of the herpes simplex virus, allowing for a faster recovery time. As a result, applying chamomile cream topically can provide quick relief from cold sore symptoms, helping to heal the sores faster and reduce their appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and redness associated with cold sores, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antiviral property: Chamomile contains flavonoids and terpenoids that exhibit antiviral activity against the herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores, helping to suppress viral replication and promote healing.

Soothing and calming property: The glycosides present in chamomile have a soothing effect on mucous membranes, reducing itching, burning, and discomfort associated with cold sores, allowing for faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are used to create soothing and calming creams, providing anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and discomfort caused by cold sores.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have potent antiviral and antibacterial effects, helping to accelerate the healing process of cold sore lesions.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers measuring 2 tablespoons.
Combine the dried chamomile flowers with 1 cup of distilled water in a heat-resistant bowl and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl discarding solids and reserve liquid.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the reserved liquid stirring until smooth and melted.
Pour the herbal cream mixture into sterilized jars and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with cold sore because of its potent antiviral properties, which target the herpes simplex virus that causes the outbreak. The cream's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce viral replication and inflammation, thus soothing the affected area and reducing the duration of the cold sore. Additionally, lavender's calming and anti-inflammatory effects can help alleviate pain, discomfort, and itching associated with the outbreak, making it an effective natural remedy for managing cold sores.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: English lavender contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with cold sore lesions.

Antiviral properties: The antiviral properties of lavender may help inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak.

Soothing and calming effects: Lavender's calming and soothing properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for cold sore outbreaks, while also providing a gentle, non-irritating relief from discomfort and itching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, lavender roots help reduce inflammation and promote healing in cold sore treatment.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, lavender seeds provide anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties for skin soothing cream.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 30g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers according to botanical guidelines for drying herbs.
Grind the dried lavender flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered lavender with 100ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl, allowing it to steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass measuring cup, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the herb residue.
Mix the resulting liquid extract with 200g of beeswax and 50ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, stirring until the mixture is fully emulsified and cooled to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with cold sore because of its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint has been shown to reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks by inhibiting the replication of the herpes simplex virus, while also soothing the skin and reducing swelling. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help numb the affected area, providing instant relief from discomfort and pain. As a result, applying herbal peppermint creams to cold sores can speed up the healing process and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Peppermint creams contain antiviral compounds like menthol and menthone, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of herpes simplex virus (HSV), reducing the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores by minimizing inflammation and promoting healing.

Antimicrobial activity: The antimicrobial properties of peppermint cream, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, can help prevent secondary infections that may occur during a cold sore outbreak, allowing for faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help soothe and calm skin irritations associated with cold sores.
Rhyzomes: Containing high amounts of menthol and other bioactive compounds, peppermint rhyzomes provide analgesic and antiviral properties to reduce cold sore symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh peppermint leaves, wash them thoroughly with distilled water and pat dry with paper towels.
Combine the dried leaves with 200g of organic coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass jar and steep for 4 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, squeezing gently to extract as much oil as possible, discarding solids.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 150ml of distilled water in a double boiler until melted, then add 20ml of the infused oil and stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers, allowing it to cool and harden at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic creams helps with cold sore because of its antiviral properties, which specifically target the herpes simplex virus responsible for the outbreak. The cream's active compounds, such as allicin and ajoene, have been shown to inhibit viral replication and reduce inflammation, speeding up the healing process. Additionally, garlic's antibacterial properties help combat secondary bacterial infections that can worsen cold sore symptoms, providing quick relief from pain, itching, and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Herbal garlic creams contain antiviral compounds like allicin, which helps to combat the herpes simplex virus (HSV) that causes cold sores, reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Garlic has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with cold sore lesions, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.

Antibacterial activity: The antibacterial compounds in herbal garlic creams, such as allyl sulfides, help to combat secondary bacterial infections that can occur during a cold sore outbreak, preventing further complications and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Garlic roots contain antibacterial and antiviral compounds that help combat cold sore infections and reduce their severity.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome, or underground stem, of garlic contains sulfur compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh garlic bulbs, clean and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped garlic with 20g of beeswax pellets and 10ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Remove from heat and let it cool to 60°c, then add 5 drops of tea tree essential oil and mix well.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers, label and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with cold sore because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root that has potent antiviral properties. When applied topically to the affected area, glycyrrhizin can help shorten the duration and alleviate the symptoms of cold sores, including pain, itching, and inflammation. Additionally, licorice creams may also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe the skin and reduce redness, promoting a faster recovery from the infection.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores.

Licoricidin: This flavonoid has been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), which is responsible for causing cold sores, thereby reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.

Saponins: These natural compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and preventing bacterial or fungal infections that can worsen cold sore symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help soothe and heal cold sore lesions.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, helping to combat infections associated with cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra root with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a double boiler to create an infusion that takes 30 minutes to prepare.
Mix 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax with the infused glycyrrhiza glabra powder, heating the mixture at 60°c for 15 minutes.
Add 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil and 2 milliliters of aloe vera gel to the mixture, stirring until fully incorporated and taking an additional 10 minutes to prepare.
Pour the cream into sterilized tubes or containers, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with cold sore because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the skin. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial compounds inhibit the growth of the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores. Additionally, comfrey's natural moisturizing and emollient qualities promote healing and regeneration of skin tissue, reducing the appearance of scars and discoloration caused by cold sore outbreaks. This natural cream provides gentle, non-irritating relief for affected areas, helping to speed up the recovery process and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey contains compounds like allantoin, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with cold sore outbreaks, making it easier to manage discomfort and speed up recovery.

Wound-healing properties: Comfrey's high concentration of allantoin, vitamin B12, and other nutrients promotes wound healing by stimulating collagen production, enhancing tissue repair, and protecting the skin from further damage.

Antiviral properties: Comfrey contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including herpes simplex virus 1 (HSV-1), the primary cause of cold sores.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots contain allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with cold sores.
Stems: The stems of the comfrey plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has potent antiviral properties that help to combat the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated ensuring optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation at room temperature for three days or less to preserve volatile compounds.
Combine dried symphytum officinale leaves with organic coconut oil in a ratio of 1 part herb to 5 parts carrier oil stirring thoroughly to ensure homogenization.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or warm water bath at 160°f for one hour to facilitate decarboxylation and infusion of herbal compounds.
Strain the cooled infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into glass jars storing them in the refrigerator for up to six months with topical application as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with cold sore because it contains a unique combination of herbal extracts that work synergistically to combat the underlying causes of cold sores. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial agents help eliminate the herpes virus responsible for the outbreak. Additionally, the cream's soothing and moisturizing ingredients provide relief from discomfort and dryness, promoting faster healing and reducing the likelihood of future breakouts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as eucalyptol and caryophyllene, can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sore outbreaks.

Antiviral properties: The cream's antiviral compounds, including eugenol and globulol, may help inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores, reducing the duration and severity of outbreaks.

Wound-healing properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's wound-healing compounds, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, can accelerate the healing process by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue oxygenation, and enhancing immune response.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm cold sore symptoms.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antibacterial and antiviral activity, making them effective against the herpes simplex virus.
Seeds: Packed with omega-6 fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair in cold sore lesions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered eucalyptus globulus with 100ml of coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass beaker, allowing for gentle heat transfer.
Combine the mixture with 20g of beeswax pellets and 10g of vitamin e oil, stirring until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c.
Pour the mixture into a lip balm tube or tin, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature over a period of two hours.
Once solidified, store the cream in an airtight container at a temperature below 20°c for up to six months, applying a thin layer as needed to affected areas three to five times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with cold sore because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling. The active compound gingerol in ginger also has antiviral properties that work to combat the herpes simplex virus responsible for causing cold sores. Additionally, ginger's warming sensation can promote blood flow to the affected area, speeding up the healing process and reducing pain and discomfort associated with cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce swelling and pain associated with cold sores by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antiviral properties: The antiviral compounds in ginger, including 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores, reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: Ginger's analgesic compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with cold sores by blocking pain receptors and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, the underground stem of the ginger plant, contains bioactive compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in soothing and treating cold sores.

Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain a high concentration of starch, which is converted into glucose during processing, providing a natural humectant that helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry skin affected by cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh ginger roots and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Grind the ginger roots into a fine paste using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250ml of distilled water with 20g of grated beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler until melted.
Combine the ginger paste with 30ml of coconut oil and 10ml of jojoba oil then add it to the melted wax mixture stirring well.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for at least 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce the severity and duration of cold sore symptoms by reducing inflammation, pain, and swelling. Additionally, turmeric's antiviral properties help combat the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of recurrence. By applying a turmeric cream directly to affected areas, individuals can experience relief from cold sore discomfort and promote a healthier skin environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds (curcuminoids): Reduces swelling, pain, and redness associated with cold sores by inhibiting inflammatory enzymes and suppressing immune responses.
Antiviral activity: Inhibits the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores, thereby preventing the spread of the infection and reducing the duration of symptoms.
Wound-healing properties: Accelerates the healing process by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and enhancing immune responses to promote faster resolution of cold sore lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce cold sore symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of curcumin, which inhibits herpes simplex virus replication and reduces inflammation associated with cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered turmeric with 200ml of distilled water in a heat-proof bowl and stir until it forms a smooth paste.
Add 50g of beeswax, 20g of coconut oil, and 10g of shea butter to the turmeric paste and melt the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and add 5 drops of tea tree essential oil and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel, stirring until well combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized tubes or jars and let it cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with cold sore because they provide a soothing and protective barrier for the affected area, allowing the skin to heal quickly. The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallows help reduce redness and swelling, while their moisturizing effects keep the area hydrated and calm. Additionally, the antioxidants in herbal marshmallow creams may help combat the virus that causes cold sores, reducing its severity and duration. By applying a marshmallow cream to the affected area, individuals can experience faster healing and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow cream's mucilages, such as polysaccharides and glycoproteins, help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and pain associated with cold sore outbreaks.
Antioxidants: The antioxidant properties of marshmallow creams can help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate cold sore symptoms, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring.

Soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow cream's soothing agents, such as saponins and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help to reduce swelling, redness, and discomfort associated with cold sores.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which soothe and calm the skin, making them an effective ingredient in treating cold sore symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marigold flowers and dry them under shade for at least two weeks or until crispy.
Measure 100g of dried marigold flowers and combine with 500ml of cold pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Place the jar in a warm dark place and allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking occasionally, as the mixture infuses slowly.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Mix 10% of the infused oil with 90% of cold cream and add a few drops of vitamin e oil as an antioxidant stabilizer for shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with cold sore because it contains hyperforin, a compound that has potent antiviral properties. When applied topically to the affected area, this cream can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sore symptoms. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant actions also soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, St John's Wort's antimicrobial properties prevent bacterial infection, promoting a faster recovery from the cold sore.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have antiviral properties, specifically against herpes simplex virus type 1 (HSV-1), which is responsible for causing cold sores. By inhibiting viral replication, hyperforin helps reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.

Flavonoids: St. John's Wort creams contain flavonoids, a class of plant compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Flavonoids can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with cold sores.

Tannins: Tannins are another group of plant compounds present in St. John's Wort creams, which possess astringent and anti-inflammatory effects. Tannins can help dry out and constrict the cold sore blister, reducing its size and discomfort, while also preventing bacterial infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help reduce redness and itching associated with cold sores.
Flowers: Rich in hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the replication of the herpes virus, reducing the severity of cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers to obtain sufficient quantity for the recipe.
Combine 20g of dried hypericum perforatum herbs with 100ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass jar.
Place the mixture in a dark cupboard for 4 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow infusion time.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container and discard solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 100ml of the infused oil and heat gently until fully incorporated and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with cold sore because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and swelling. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to hydrate the skin, speeding up the healing process. Additionally, valerian root is known for its antiviral properties, which can help to combat the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores, reducing the duration and severity of outbreaks. This natural approach can provide relief from the discomfort and embarrassment associated with cold sores.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and anti-viral activity: Valerian cream's anti-inflammatory and anti-viral properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores, while also combating the virus that causes them.
Wound healing and tissue repair: Valerian's natural wound-healing properties promote faster recovery from cold sore blisters, reducing scarring and promoting healthy skin regeneration.

Pain relief and analgesic activity: Valerian's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds help alleviate itching, burning, and stinging sensations associated with cold sores, providing relief from discomfort and distress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the valerian plant contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making it effective in reducing cold sore symptoms.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including cold sores, due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valeriana officinalis root extract and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the powdered valeriana officinalis root with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to extract the active compounds from the herbal extract.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids, then add 200 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil and stir until fully incorporated.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with cold sore because they harness the natural antiviral properties of the plant's leaves. The cream absorbs quickly into the skin, directly targeting the HSV-1 virus that causes cold sores. As the virus is neutralized, inflammation and discomfort are reduced, allowing the skin to heal faster and more effectively. Additionally, the cream's antibacterial properties help prevent secondary infections and promote a healthy, pain-free recovery from cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_properties: Adiantum majus extract: This ingredient has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which helps to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores.
Glycerin: As a humectant, glycerin attracts and retains moisture in the skin, soothing dryness and cracking caused by cold sore blisters, while also creating a protective barrier against irritants.
Vitamin E: With its antioxidant properties, vitamin E helps to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate cold sore symptoms, promoting faster healing and reducing scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with cold sores.
Barks: Contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which exhibit antiviral activity against herpes simplex virus, the causative agent of cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and rinse them gently under running water.
Combine the leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 24 hours at room temperature allowing the ginkgo to infuse its properties into the oil.
Strain the oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after 1 hour of pressing.
Mix the filtered oil with 2% beeswax and 0.5% vitamin e oil and stir until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with cold sore because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce the pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with cold sore outbreaks. The cream's mucilage, a thick, protective substance, forms a barrier on the skin that helps to lock in moisture and protect the affected area from further irritation. Additionally, slippery elm has natural antibacterial and antiviral properties that may help to combat the underlying causes of the cold sore, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring or infection.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Slippery elm's mucilage property helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cold sore outbreaks.
Antiviral activity: Some studies have shown that slippery elm contains antiviral compounds that may help prevent the replication of the herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores.
Wound healing properties: Slippery elm's mucilage and other compounds may also promote wound healing by reducing inflammation, preventing bacterial growth, and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant contain mucilages, which provide soothing and calming properties to help reduce inflammation and pain associated with cold sores.
Barks: The barks of the slippery elm plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat the virus causing cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ulmus rubra leaves at dawn when dew is still present on the leaves for maximum potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator at low heat for 6 hours.
Combine 30 grams of dried ulmus rubra leaves with 150 milliliters of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion and extraction.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth into another container, discard the solids and add 20 grams of beeswax to the remaining liquid, then heat until melted and mixed thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness caused by the virus. The antiviral compounds in sage also inhibit the growth and replication of the herpes simplex virus, allowing the skin to heal faster and reducing the risk of scarring. Additionally, sage's antioxidant properties help soothe and calm the skin, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with cold sores.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Sage contains compounds like camphor and thujone, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), the causative agent of cold sores.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's flavonoids and phenolic acids help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with cold sores by suppressing inflammatory responses in the skin.

Antimicrobial properties: Sage's essential oils possess antimicrobial activity against various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate or cause secondary infections during a cold sore outbreak.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm cold sore symptoms.
Leaves: Sage leaves are rich in antioxidants and terpenes, which can reduce inflammation, combat bacterial and viral infections, and accelerate the healing process of cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered herb with 20 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of sweet almond oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and gently warm it over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool to room temperature, then stir in 10 grams of aloe vera gel and 5 drops of tea tree essential oil.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or until needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with cold sore because of its antiviral and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to target the HSV-1 virus that causes cold sores, reducing its ability to replicate and spread. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sore outbreaks. As a result, herbal thyme creams can provide rapid relief and promote faster healing of cold sores.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Thyme contains compounds like thymol, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), helping to reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in thyme possess anti-inflammatory activities, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with cold sores.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds have been shown to combat secondary bacterial infections that can occur as a result of cold sore outbreaks, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have antibacterial and antiviral properties, making them effective in reducing the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.
Leaves: Thyme leaves contain flavonoids, which help soothe and calm inflamed skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried thyme leaves with olive oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard solids.
Combine the strained oil with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler on low heat.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax melts and the temperature reaches 160-170°f, taking approximately 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30-40 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for cold sore are topical ointments containing natural ingredients extracted from plants, herbs, and botanicals, which provide relief and healing to cold sore lesions. These creams help to reduce inflammation, soothe pain, and accelerate the recovery process by boosting the body's immune system. Examples of herbal creams that effectively treat cold sores include those containing aloe vera, tea tree oil, and lemon balm. By using these creams, individuals can alleviate the discomfort and embarrassment associated with cold sores, allowing them to resume their normal activities and improve overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cold sore with herbal creams is by applying a cream containing natural ingredients such as aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile. These herbs have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores. Look for creams that are free from harsh chemicals and artificial fragrances to ensure effective treatment without irritating the skin.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cold sore that are treated with herbal creams are fever blisters, canker sores, and herpes simplex. These conditions cause painful lesions on the skin or mucous membranes, often triggered by stress, sun exposure, or hormonal fluctuations. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and calendula can help soothe and calm these symptoms, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
